title: United States Passport Renewal by Mail (Form DS-82)
description: Renewal of a U.S. passport book and/or card by mail for eligible adults using Department of State Form DS-82. This document describes the eligibility gate, the applicant inputs, and the by-mail flow. It does not submit the form; the live travel.state.gov source is always authoritative (see verification).

authority.name: U.S. Department of State, Bureau of Consular Affairs

source.notes: Eligibility conditions and required submission items derived from the official 'Renew Your Passport by Mail' page on travel.state.gov. Field-level inputs reflect Form DS-82. See registry/us/dos/passport-renewal-ds82/1.0.0/VERIFICATION.md for the source-review record.

fields.label: Has your most recent passport ever been reported lost or stolen?
fields.helpText: If your most recent passport was reported lost or stolen, you cannot renew by mail with Form DS-82 and must apply in person.
fields.source: Eligibility: passport never reported lost or stolen

fields.label: Was your most recent passport issued within the last 15 years?
fields.helpText: Count from the issue date printed on your passport to today. A passport issued more than 15 years ago is not eligible for renewal by mail.
fields.source: Eligibility: passport issued within the last 15 years

fields.label: Were you age 16 or older when your most recent passport was issued, and was it valid for 10 years?
fields.helpText: Passports issued to applicants under age 16 (and other limited-validity passports) generally cannot be renewed by mail. You cannot renew a passport for a child under 16.
fields.source: Eligibility: passport issued at age 16 or older and valid for 10 years

fields.label: Can you submit your most recent passport with your application?
fields.helpText: Your most recent passport must be mailed in with the application.
fields.source: Eligibility: can submit most recent passport

fields.label: Was your passport issued in your current name, or can you document a name change?
fields.helpText: Answer yes if the name on your passport is your current name, or if you can provide a certified marriage certificate, divorce decree, or court order showing the change.
fields.source: Eligibility: issued in current name or name-change document available

fields.label: Is your passport undamaged (other than normal wear and tear)?
fields.helpText: A damaged passport (beyond normal wear) cannot be renewed by mail and must be replaced in person.
fields.source: Eligibility: passport not damaged other than normal wear and tear

fields.label: Last name (as it should appear on the passport)
Place of birth (city and state, or city and country)
Sex marker to appear on the passport
Social Security number
fields.helpText: Enter your 9-digit U.S. Social Security number. Federal law requires it for a passport application; if you have never been issued one, the live form provides an alternative attestation.

fields.label: State or territory
fields.helpText: Two-letter USPS state or territory abbreviation, e.g. CA, NY, TX.

fields.label: Full name as printed on your most recent passport
fields.source: DS-82 — Most Recent Passport: Name as printed

fields.label: Most recent passport number
fields.helpText: The number printed on the passport book or card you are renewing. U.S. passport numbers are typically 9 characters; older books may be all digits and newer ones may begin with a letter.
fields.source: DS-82 — Most Recent Passport: Passport Number

fields.label: Issue date of your most recent passport
fields.source: DS-82 — Most Recent Passport: Issue Date

fields.label: Name-change document (only if your current name differs from the passport)
fields.helpText: A certified copy of a marriage certificate, divorce decree, or court order. Required only if the name on your most recent passport is not your current name.

fields.label: Passport photo
fields.helpText: One color photograph meeting the published U.S. passport photo requirements (2x2 inches, plain background, taken within the last 6 months).

fields.label: Processing speed
fields.helpText: Expedited service costs an additional fee and is faster than routine service. Current processing times and fees are published on the live source.

fields.label: Payment method
fields.helpText: Renewals by mail are paid by personal check or money order payable to the U.S. Department of State. Confirm the current fee on the live source.
fields.source: Required submission: payment by check or money order

steps.title: Check you can renew by mail
steps.description: Form DS-82 (renew by mail) is available only when every eligibility condition is met. If any condition fails, you must apply in person.

steps.title: You must apply in person
steps.description: Based on your answers you are not eligible to renew by mail with Form DS-82 (for example: passport lost or stolen, issued more than 15 years ago, issued before age 16, damaged, or a name change you cannot document). You must apply in person; the live source explains how.
